steps.title: Identify the subject and temporal signal
steps.content: The subject is “her painting” (singular), and “Currently” signals present time.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and present tense
steps.content: A verb must agree in number with its subject and be in the appropriate tense. B) hangs is present tense (matching “Currently”) and singular. The other three use wrong tenses: hung (A) is simple past, was hanging (C) is past continuous, and will have hung (D) is future perfect—incompatible with “Currently”. That matches choice B — B is correct.
